title: Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards: government response to report
description: The government's response to the Committee's 2020 report Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards
summary: The government sets out its approach to implementing the recommendations made in the 2020 report on Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards. This response document outlines the government's plans to improve public trust in AI by establishing clearer standards and regulations.

title: Report and impact assessment on Copyright and Artificial Intelligence
description: A report and impact assessment on the use of copyright works in the development of artificial intelligence (AI) systems.
summary: This report examines the impact of using copyrighted works in the development of artificial intelligence systems. It assesses the implications for copyright law and potentially affects how AI systems utilize existing copyright materials.

title: Generative artificial intelligence (AI) in education
description: The Department for Education’s (DfE) position on the use of generative AI in the education sector.
summary: The Department for Education is considering the use of generative AI in education, and this policy outlines their position and guidelines on its implementation. The policy aims to ensure the responsible use of generative AI in the education sector.

title: Transport artificial intelligence action plan
description: Sets out how DfT will work with the transport sector to exploit the opportunities from AI while managing potential risks.
summary: This government policy outlines a strategic plan for using artificial intelligence in the transport sector, aiming to seize its benefits while minimizing potential risks. It will involve collaboration between the Department for Transport and the transport industry to advance AI technology safely and effectively.

title: Copyright and artificial intelligence: progress report
description: A progress report on the use of copyright works in the development of AI systems, under Section 137 of the Data (Use and Access) Act.
summary: The data protection regulator has published a progress report on using copyright works to develop artificial intelligence systems. The report is an update on progress under the Data (Use and Access) Act, specifically section 137.

title: The regulation of artificial intelligence as a medical device: government response to the RHC
description: Government response to the recommendations made by the Regulatory Horizons Council (RHC) on the regulation of AI as a medical device.
summary: The government has created a policy to regulate artificial intelligence used in medical devices in line with recommendations from a council. This policy aims to ensure the safe and effective use of AI in medical settings.

title: ​​Ofqual’s approach to regulating the use of artificial intelligence in the qualifications sector​
description: Published in response to a request from the Secretary of State for Education and the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology.
summary: Ofqual is creating a policy to regulate the use of artificial intelligence in qualifications, such as exams and assessments. This policy aims to ensure that AI is used fairly and safely in education.

title: Government response to the House of Lords Select Committee on Artificial Intelligence
description: Government response to the Lords Select Committee on Artificial Intelligence report ‘AI in the UK: No Room for Complacency’.
summary: The UK government is responding to recommendations made by a House of Lords Select Committee on Artificial Intelligence. The response aims to address the potential risks and opportunities of AI in the UK, following the report 'AI in the UK: No Room for Complacency'.

title: AI and Public Standards – Terms of Reference
description: Terms of Reference and FAQs for the Committee's review into artificial intelligence and public standards.
summary: The "AI and Public Standards – Terms of Reference" document outlines the Terms of Reference and FAQs for a committee reviewing the impact of artificial intelligence on public standards. This review aims to provide a framework for consideration and guidance on the development and implementation of public standards related to artificial intelligence.

title: CSPL submission to Science and Technology Committee inquiry on Governance of AI
description: CSPL has submitted written evidence to the Science and Technology Committee's inquiry on Governance of Artificial Intelligence.
summary: The CSPL (Committee on Standards in Public Life) has submitted its views on regulating Artificial Intelligence to a Science and Technology Committee inquiry. This submission aims to provide guidance on the governance of AI.
